messages.text: home in 20 — orange line actually cooperating for once. you eat yet or should i grab something from the thai place
home in 20. long one today. wine's already breathing on the counter, i checked the cam lol
Dad had a rough one today. didn't know what day it was for like an hour, kept asking about mom. i'm ok, don't spiral. let's just talk Sunday. love you
eyes on, grabbing the corner table before the GW crowd floods it. ordered you the manchego. usual rule — phones face down, no shop talk
so are we doing the shenandoah thing or are you gonna 'maybe' it to death again 🙃 i can hold the airbnb til midnight then it's gone. no pressure (some pressure)

i can't keep doing this alone. two kids and a job and now i'm the only one driving Dad to neuro? you 'meant to come last month' three months running. i'm not trying to guilt you, i'm just tired. we need an actual plan
home in 20 too — orange line cooperating for once. want me to grab anything from the market? lol

awake again. reinvestigation packet's due in three weeks and i keep rereading the SF-86 like it's gonna bite me. and dad's on top of it — 11 weeks since i've been out there, erin reminds me without saying it. brain won't shut up. you up?
how's dad this week? neuro appt still on the calendar? lmk if there's anything i can take off your plate from here
home in 20. rough day, will explain later
booked columbus for next wknd to see my parents — you ok solo here? figured you've got the dad stuff on your mind anyway. love you
home in 20. and yeah i'm good solo, go see your folks — em dash i'll probably just regroup and catch up on stuff. thank you for asking

Hi —

Requesting PTO for Fri 6/26 through Mon 6/29 for a family matter in Ohio (father's care). I'll have coverage notes posted before EOD Thursday and will be reachable by phone if anything urgent comes up on the engagement.

Thanks,
Rob
trivia tonight? need to get out of my head — dad/clearance stuff stacking up. could use the regular table and a beer
running late, trivia w/ Derek. don't wait on dinner — back by 9ish

hey Dave, got a non-tech question for the family security guy. dad's alone in the bungalow and the diagnosis is moving — what's reasonable to set up for him before it gets worse without making him feel watched? you've seen this kind of thing
flights booked, in next Fri — let's tag-team the neuro appt. i'll take the doctor questions if you handle dad-wrangling, then we figure out the longer-term plan together. for real this time
heading home, long one. erin and i finally talked through the dad plan — feel a little less useless about it. love you, see you in a bit
wrapping up, home soon. orange line gods willing
calling now for the sunday check-in — want to lock down the friday logistics and actually split this up. it's been a lot carrying it from cincy. need you present this time, ok?

morning — quick one: I've got PTO in for this Fri–Sun, just want to confirm it's cleared before I book travel. Family thing in Ohio, nothing dramatic, but I'd rather not be reachable Fri afternoon. Coverage on the Crystal City ticket is in the handoff doc. lmk and thanks.
got it, home in 20 — orange line cooperating for once. don't start dinner without me, I'm making the thing you like
ok let's lock Friday. what time is the neurology appt — and are you driving Dad or do you need me to? I land midday so I can take him if that's easier on you. just tell me what helps.

awake again, of course. reinvestigation paperwork sitting open in one tab and my sister's texts about Dad in the other and I can't make either of them quieter. flying out Friday. he forgot which year it was on the phone last week and I just… kept talking like I didn't notice. you ever feel like you're cleared to handle the whole country's secrets but not one old man losing his?

can't sleep. weirdly nervous about this trip — not the flight, just what I'll find when I get there. thank you for taking the apartment stuff this week, and for not making me explain why I've been quiet. means more than I'm saying.
sitting trivia out tomorrow — flying to Columbus first thing, Dad's gotten worse. drink one for me. check fire on the Whitlow's bench seat, I want it back next week.

confirmed, 2pm Friday. you'll meet us there? I'll get him fed and dressed in the morning. and hey — I know I've left most of this on you. I'm not pretending otherwise. we'll figure out the rest together this weekend.
at the gate, DCA. boarding soon. I'll FaceTime you tonight once I've got eyes on him and know what we're dealing with. love you. hold down the fort.

[FaceTime, ~22 min] Recap — he's worse than Erin let on. Didn't recognize the kitchen layout he installed himself. Mail unopened on the counter for weeks. Voice tight the whole call; Maya mostly listening. Ends with 'I don't think he can keep living here alone and I don't know how to say that out loud yet.'

[Phone call, ~18 min] Calls Dave for steady non-technical counsel — not security, just 'you've watched people make this call before.' Asks how you know when keeping someone home stops being kindness. Dave: 'You'll know. You already do. Question is whether you can forgive yourself for it.' He doesn't answer.

[FaceTime, ~30 min] Emotionally drained recap. Spent the day touring two memory-care places and sitting with Dad's confusion. Long silences. Admits guilt that it's been 11 weeks since he last visited. Maya gently reminds him this isn't his to carry alone; he nods, doesn't quite believe it.

thank you for today — and for the last year I wasn't here for. I mean it. let's split the legwork on facilities: I'll take the two Clintonville-side places and the financials, you take the ones near you and the medical coordination. shared doc tonight. we do this as a team from here.

[Quiet evening at home, in person] Back in Arlington. Talks the whole thing through with Maya on the couch — the facility decision, the guilt, what he owes Erin, what he owes his dad. First time he says the engagement-shaped thing out loud sideways: 'this week made me think a lot about what I keep putting off.' Doesn't finish it. She lets it sit.

hey — been thinking about the paperwork. what if i take POA (financial/legal) and you keep healthcare proxy since you're closest to his doctors? feels like the cleanest split. you've carried too much of the day-to-day already. lmk and i'll get the forms started this week.

low key drowning over here. dad's slipping faster than the neuro said he would and the reinvestigation window opens next month. lovely timing. anyway — eyes on, i'm fine. just venting into the void. trivia thursday still on?

can we actually talk through the facility thing tonight when you're home? i keep spiraling on it in my head and it goes nowhere. you're better at this than me — i just need to say it out loud to someone who isn't erin or my own brain at 3am. no rush, after you decompress.
home around 9 — trivia w derek. don't wait on dinner. love you

morning dave — got 20 min later? wanted to pick your brain on a couple non-tech things w dad. the facility question, and how erin and i even start the conversation about him not driving anymore. you've seen this stuff. no pressure if it's a bad day.

talked to uncle dave. he says start with a 'trial' respite stay so it doesn't feel permanent to dad, and let the doctor be the one to pull the license — not us. takes the bad-guy role off our plate. paperwork split confirmed: i've got POA, you take MyChart + proxy. i'll mail you the signature pages. thank you for everything, seriously.

that was exactly what i needed tonight. good day for once — dad was lucid on the phone, even cracked a joke about the HVAC in the new place. felt almost normal. thank you for not letting me carry it alone. asleep already? :)

dad had a rough one today. didn't know what year it was, got upset, asked for mom twice. i handled it but i'm running on empty. i know you're busy out there but it would mean a lot if you came home before the next crisis instead of after. just saying.
erin's text gutted me. she's not wrong and that's the part that stings. gonna try to actually sleep tonight instead of doing the 2am thing. lights out? come here.

hey. dad had a rough one tonight — got confused around dinner, kept asking where mom was, then got upset when I reminded him. wandered to the front door twice. I really think we're past the point where he's safe alone in that house. can we PLEASE actually talk about Bickford or one of the memory care places before something happens? I can't keep being the only one driving up there. sunday call, for real this time?

you up? course you are lol. can't sleep. dad's neuro consult is in the morning and erin basically issued an ultimatum tonight — facility tour, the whole thing. and the timing is just *perfect* because my reinvestigation window opens next month and the last thing I need is "applicant under significant family/financial stress" flagged on the 86. I keep doing the math on memory care costs vs the RSUs and it makes me feel like a ghoul. anyway. didn't sign up to be the eldest son this decade. how's your week, you holding up

ok consult done. neuro confirmed progression, moderate now, said unsupervised living is no longer advisable — so you were right, I'm not going to pretend otherwise. we got the durable POA / proxy split sorted, I'll take medical-financial coordination, you stay primary on-the-ground since you're closest. we're touring facilities this week, he's pulling a shortlist near you. thank you for carrying this. I mean it. I'll be up more, I promise.

hey — that's done. kind of. consult went how we figured, he can't stay in the house. erin and I actually agreed on something for once, miracle. touring places this week. I'm wrung out but weirdly relieved that there's a plan now instead of just dread? anyway. don't want to think about it for a few hours. you still want to do the thai place tonight, I could really use you and a glass of wine and zero decisions
